close_reason: Accepted SDK gap. Already has ZEROIZATION GAP comment at the call site. The bitwarden SDK requires an owned String for AccessTokenLoginRequest — no workaround exists.
description: In secretx-bitwarden/src/lib.rs (around line 154), build_authed_client() passes the machine account access token to the Bitwarden SDK via: access_token: access_token.to_string(). This creates a plain String owned by the SDK that is not under our Zeroizing control. The field self.access_token is Zeroizing<String>, but .to_string() clones the content into a non-zeroizing allocation before handing it to the SDK. This is an SDK-imposed limitation with no clean fix at our layer, but it should be documented with a ZEROIZATION GAP comment identical to those used elsewhere in the project, so the gap is visible to auditors and not silently present.
title: bitwarden build_authed_client() copies access token into non-Zeroizing SDK String
